claims.claim_text: Structural market dynamics driven by derivatives, centralized positioning, and systematic flows have made markets reflexively volatile in both directions, disconnecting price action from fundamentals.
reasoning_chain: Step 1: Zero-days-to-expiry options and centralized asset management create herding behavior among hedge funds, amplifying moves beyond what fundamentals justify.
Step 2: Retail participation in options (mass put-buying at lows, aggressive call-buying at highs) further exacerbates momentum in both directions.
Step 3: The resulting 99th-percentile 10-day rally was driven not by fundamental improvement but by positioning unwind and dollar weakness, making the move unreliable as a signal.
Step 4: This structure means extreme readings (retail in 90th percentile call-buying) are fade signals, not confirmation signals.

claims.claim_text: Inflation is structurally sticky and will not return to target because tariffs, oil, and loss of shelter disinflation tailwinds are combining simultaneously, while the Fed is unlikely to get ahead of it.
reasoning_chain: Step 1: Core goods inflation was already accelerating pre-tariffs; tariffs have pushed core goods PCE to ~2.5%, preventing the deflation that would otherwise have occurred.
Step 2: Shelter inflation disinflation tailwind (owner's equivalent rent) has normalized — this key disinflationary driver is exhausted.
Step 3: Oil remains elevated relative to pre-conflict levels, adding to headline inflation pressure.
Step 4: The regime has shifted from a 2% ceiling to a 2% floor on inflation — getting to 2% is now the bottom of the range, not the target.
Step 5: The Fed is politically constrained (Trump seeking compliant Fed chair) and unlikely to tighten sufficiently; Treasury liquidity management (coupon reduction) bypasses Fed hawkishness.
Step 6: Fiscal stimulus likely to increase pre-midterms, adding further inflationary impulse.
